title: PC-1.4: Quote Locking — Atomic checkout prevents concurrent edits
description: Prevent concurrent edits to same quote. Add locked_by, locked_at, lock_expires_at to quote table. Atomic claim via UPDATE WHERE locked_by IS NULL OR lock_expires_at < NOW(). Return 409 Conflict with current owner. Auto-expire stale locks (30 min). Force-unlock for admins with audit logging. MCP: quote_lock/quote_unlock. Inspired by Paperclip's atomic issue checkout.

title: EPIC-V2-ANOMALY: Pricing Anomaly Detection
description:
## Overview
Before a quote finalizes, the system automatically flags unusual patterns: discount way above customer's historical average, margin below floor for the product category, configuration that's valid but unusual, price that differs significantly from similar deals.

## Why This Matters
Catches mistakes before they become problems. Sales reps will trust the system more because it catches errors. Reduces pricing errors that cost money. Provides early warning on unusual deals that might need extra scrutiny.

## Background
Traditional CPQ validates against rules but doesn't detect patterns. This adds ML-style anomaly detection (but deterministic, not actual ML) to surface unusual quotes for review.

## Technical Considerations
- Historical baseline: calculate average discount/margin per customer segment
- Standard deviation alerts: flag quotes > 2 std dev from norm
- Product combo detection: unusual product combinations
- Price comparison: compare to similar deals (same products, segment, region)
- Configurable thresholds: different segments can have different sensitivity
- Alert UI: show flagged items with explanation
- Override capability: rep can override with justification

## Anomaly Types
1. **Discount anomaly**: discount significantly higher than customer's history
2. **Margin anomaly**: margin below floor for product category
3. **Config anomaly**: valid but unusual configuration (e.g., huge quantity)
4. **Price anomaly**: total significantly different from similar deals
5. **Velocity anomaly**: quote created very quickly (might be automated gaming)

## User Experience
When rep tries to finalize quote:
```
⚠️ Quote flagged for review

- Discount (18%) is 2.3x customer's average (7.8%)
- Margin (52%) is below SaaS floor (60%)
- First time ordering "Onboarding" add-on

[ Override with Justification ] [ Adjust Quote ] [ View Similar Deals ]
```

## Goals
1. Detect discount anomalies
2. Detect margin anomalies
3. Detect configuration anomalies
4. Detect price anomalies
5. Show reasoning for flags
6. Allow override with justification
7. Audit trail of overrides

## Dependencies
- Historical quote data (exists in v1)
- Pricing engine (exists in v1)
- Policy engine (exists in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #6]

title: EPIC-V2-MCP: MCP Server for AI Agents
description:
## Overview
Make Quotey controllable by AI coding agents via Model Context Protocol (MCP). AI agents can programmatically query pricing, create quotes, check approvals, and trigger document generation.

## Why This Matters
Every other CPQ tool requires human interaction only. AI agents become internal users, enabling AI-powered quoting workflows. This is a critical differentiator - no competitor offers this. Opens new use cases: automated deal scoring, AI quote optimization, programmatic quote generation.

## Background
MCP is an open protocol enabling AI agents to connect to tools and services. By exposing Quotey via MCP, we enable Claude Code and other AI agents to create quotes programmatically, automated workflows from external systems, and integration with AI-powered CRM systems.

## Technical Considerations
- MCP server implementation in Rust (use existing MCP SDK or build custom)
- Define tool schema for each Quotey capability following MCP conventions
- Support API key and OAuth authentication
- Handle rate limiting and error responses properly
- Ensure idempotency for agent retries
- Full audit trail for ALL agent actions (critical for enterprise)

## Goals
1. Agents can query product catalog programmatically
2. Agents can create and price quotes
3. Agents can check approval status and trigger approval flows
4. Agents can trigger PDF generation
5. Full audit trail exists for all agent actions

## Dependencies
None - foundational capability, can start immediately

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #1]

Core sync. Keeps CRM up to date.

- Error handling with retry

title: TASK: Design requirement extraction prompt
description:
## Overview
Design the LLM prompt that extracts structured requirements from unstructured text.

## Why This Matters
Core of the auto-quote feature. Prompt quality determines extraction accuracy.

## Prompt Requirements
- Extract: products, quantities, features, billing preferences
- Identify ambiguities
- Confidence scoring
- Return structured JSON

## Output Format
```json
{
  "requirements": [
    {
      "type": "product",
      "name": "Pro Plan",
      "quantity": 150,
      "confidence": 0.95
    }
  ],
  "ambiguities": [
    {
      "text": "enterprise tier",
      "question": "Did you mean Pro or Enterprise?",
      "options": ["Pro Plan", "Enterprise Plan"]
    }
  ],
  "missing_info": ["start date"]
}
```

title: PC-4.1: Enhanced Audit Trail — actor model + before/after details
description:
## Objective
Upgrade audit_event fidelity to capture who did what, against which entity, with structured before/after details and causal context.

## Background / Why
Paperclip’s activity log schema encodes actor/entity/action with rich metadata, enabling robust postmortems and governance review. Quotey needs this depth to support CLO/NXT/RGN and enterprise compliance narratives.

## Scope
- Introduce explicit actor_type/actor_id and entity_type/entity_id dimensions
- Add structured detail payloads for diffs, policy checks, and approval paths
- Standardize event action taxonomy across Slack/MCP/CLI flows

## Guardrails
- Audit log remains append-only
- Event schema changes stay backward readable for existing records

## Acceptance
- Key quote mutations include before/after payloads
- Actor and entity dimensions are consistently populated
- Query tooling can filter by actor, entity, and action deterministically

title: PC-4.2: Live Quote Update Events — streaming change notifications
description:
## Objective
Publish live quote lifecycle events so Slack surfaces, portals, and operator tools can react to state changes without polling.

## Background / Why
Paperclip broadcasts live events for orchestration visibility. Quotey currently depends mostly on request/response paths, which limits operator awareness and reactive automation.

## Scope
- Add internal event bus abstraction for quote/approval/audit transitions
- Support at least one streaming transport (SSE or WebSocket)
- Emit deterministic, replay-friendly event payloads

## Guardrails
- Events must not become the source of truth (SQLite state remains canonical)
- Event publication failures cannot corrupt transactional writes

## Acceptance
- Core lifecycle transitions emit events
- Subscribers receive ordered, typed payloads
- Reconnect/resume semantics are documented and tested

title: TASK: Design similarity scoring algorithm
description:
## Overview
Design the algorithm that determines which products to suggest for a customer.

## Why This Matters
The core of the recommendation engine. Must balance relevance with diversity. Should be explainable.

## Algorithm Components
1. **Customer similarity**: Find similar customers (segment, industry, size)
2. **Purchase patterns**: What did similar customers buy?
3. **Product relationships**: Bundles, add-ons, replacements
4. **Time factors**: Seasonal trends, recent popularity
5. **Business rules**: Always suggest X for segment Y

## Scoring Formula
```
score = (similar_customer_buy * 0.4) +
        (product_relationship * 0.3) +
        (time_decay * 0.2) +
        (business_rule_boost * 0.1)
```

## Output
Algorithm design doc with:
- Component definitions
- Scoring formula
- Weight rationale
- Examples

[Created from: ultrathink - EPIC-V2-SUGGEST subtask]

title: EPIC-V2-CRM: One-Click CRM Sync
description:
## Overview
Bidirectional sync with Salesforce/HubSpot - quote status changes flow back to the CRM opportunity, deal amount updates automatically, customer data stays fresh. No manual re-entry. Single click to connect.

## Why This Matters
The #1 friction point in CPQ is data entry. Reps quote in Quotey but then have to manually update Salesforce/HubSpot. This eliminates double data entry. Deal data is always current in both systems.

## Background
v1 has Composio integration stubbed. This makes it production-ready with one-click connect.

## Technical Considerations
- OAuth flow: one-click connect to Salesforce/HubSpot
- Sync direction:
  - Quotey → CRM: quote created, status changed, finalized, approved
  - CRM → Quotey: account updated, opportunity stage changed
- Field mapping: configurable mapping between Quotey and CRM fields
- Conflict resolution: CRM wins or Quotey wins (configurable)
- Sync frequency: real-time (webhooks) or batch (hourly)
- Error handling: retry with backoff, alert on failure
- Audit trail: log all sync actions

## Sync Events

### Quotey → CRM
- Quote created → create opportunity line item
- Quote finalized → update opportunity amount, stage
- Quote approved → post to CRM
- Quote rejected → post to CRM
- Quote expired → update opportunity

### CRM → Quotey
- Account updated → sync to Quotey account
- Opportunity stage changed → update quote context
- Contact added → sync to Quotey contacts

## User Experience
Initial setup:
```
/quotey crm connect

Select CRM:
[ Salesforce ] [ HubSpot ]

→ Redirects to OAuth
→ Returns with connection confirmed

[ Configure Field Mapping ] [ Done ]
```

Ongoing:
```
Quote Q-2026-0042 finalized.
→ Syncing to Salesforce...
→ ✓ Opportunity updated (amount: $20,400)
→ ✓ Line items synced
```

## Goals
1. One-click OAuth connect to Salesforce
2. One-click OAuth connect to HubSpot
3. Bidirectional quote sync
4. Configurable field mapping
5. Real-time sync via webhooks
6. Batch sync fallback
7. Error handling with retry
8. Full sync audit trail

## Dependencies
- Composio integration (v1 has stub)
- OAuth handling
- Slack integration (for status messages)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #10]

title: EPIC-V2-ANALYTICS: Built-in Analytics Dashboard
description:
## Overview
Native dashboard showing: win rate by price range, average discount by rep/segment, quote-to-close velocity, margin trends, most-quoted products, approval bottleneck analysis. No external BI tool required.

## Why This Matters
Sales leadership always asks "how are we doing on pricing?" Currently requires data team or manual analysis. This answers questions instantly. Shows pricing health at a glance. Identifies bottlenecks in approval process.

## Background
Not planned in v1. Adding native analytics on top of existing quote data.

## Technical Considerations
- Dashboard UI: charts, tables, filters
- Metrics:
  - Win rate by price range
  - Average discount by rep/segment/region
  - Quote-to-close velocity (days)
  - Margin trends over time
  - Most-quoted products
  - Approval bottleneck analysis (who delays?)
  - Discount distribution
  - Deal size distribution
- Filters: date range, segment, rep, product
- Export: CSV download
- Scheduling: weekly email digest option

## Dashboard Sections

### Pricing Health
- Average discount by segment (bar chart)
- Discount distribution (histogram)
- Margin trends (line chart)

### Velocity
- Average quote-to-close by week (line chart)
- Quotes in flight by age

### Products
- Most quoted products (table)
- Bundle attach rate

### Approvals
- Approval turnaround time by approver
- Bottleneck analysis
- Auto-approval rate

## User Experience
Rep opens "/quotey analytics" in Slack:
```
📊 Quotey Analytics

Pricing Health:
• Avg discount: 12% (↑ 2% from last month)
• Avg margin: 68%

Velocity:
• Avg quote-to-close: 4.2 days
• 12 quotes in flight

Top Products:
1. Pro Plan (45%)
2. SSO Add-on (28%)

[ View Full Dashboard ]
```

Or open dedicated dashboard URL for full view.

## Goals
1. Pricing health metrics
2. Velocity metrics
3. Product analytics
4. Approval analytics
5. Filter by date/segment/rep
6. Export to CSV
7. Scheduled digests

## Dependencies
- Quote data (exists in v1)
- Approval data (exists in v1)

title: TASK: Implement template engine with branding
description:
## Overview
Implement template system with customizable branding.

## Why This Matters
Enables white-label and customization.

## Branding Options
- Logo upload (PNG/JPG, stored in SQLite)
- Primary color (for headers, accents)
- Secondary color
- Company name
- Company address
- Contact info

title: EPIC-V2-SUGGEST: Smart Product Suggestions Engine
description:
## Overview
After a rep selects a customer, the system automatically recommends products based on what similar customers bought, the customer's industry, their current contract, seasonal trends, and cross-sell/upsell opportunities. One-click to add.

## Why This Matters
Reps spend too much time hunting through catalogs. This makes the system feel magical - it knows what the customer likely needs. Increases average deal size through intelligent cross-sell/upsell suggestions. Reduces training burden - reps don't need to memorize the entire catalog.

## Background
This is a recommendation engine built on top of historical quote data. The system learns from past successful deals and applies that pattern to new opportunities. It's not about replacing human judgment - it's about surfacing relevant options quickly.

## Technical Considerations
- Similarity scoring algorithm based on: customer segment, industry, deal size, product categories
- Collaborative filtering: "customers like this also bought..."
- Content-based: product relationships, bundles, add-ons
- Time-decay for seasonality (Q4 deals different from Q1)
- Configurable suggestion rules (e.g., always suggest onboarding for Enterprise)
- Privacy-aware: suggestions based on anonymized aggregate data, not individual customer data

## User Experience
When rep selects a customer, show 3-5 product suggestions with brief reasoning:
- "Based on Acme's industry (FinTech), we typically include SOC2 compliance add-on"
- "Enterprise customers like Acme often add premium support in year 1"
- "Similar mid-market deals included SSO (3 of last 5)"

## Goals
1. Display 3-5 relevant product suggestions per customer
2. Show reasoning for each suggestion
3. One-click add to quote
4. Learn from accepted/rejected suggestions (feedback loop)
5. Configurable suggestion rules by segment

## Dependencies
- EPIC-V2-MCP (can be independent but suggestions enhance MCP tools)
- Historical quote data (existing in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #2]

title: PC-7.1: Per-Entity Schema Documentation — executable model contracts
description:
## Objective
Create complete per-entity schema docs that encode invariants, ownership, lifecycle transitions, and migration assumptions for core Quotey domains.

## Background / Why
Paperclip’s explicit schema references reduced ambiguity across contributors. Quotey’s increasing domain depth needs equivalent contracts to avoid drift and hidden coupling.

## Scope
- Document each major entity with fields, constraints, and relationships
- Include ownership and source-of-truth notes per field
- Link entities to corresponding service/repository modules and migrations

## Guardrails
- Docs must stay synchronized with migration history
- Avoid aspirational fields that do not exist in code

## Acceptance
- Each targeted entity has a current contract doc
- Contracts include invariants and failure modes
- Reviewers can trace data ownership without code spelunking

title: PC-7.2: Service Layer Business Rules — move policy out of transport edges
description:
## Objective
Consolidate business rule enforcement into explicit service-layer boundaries, reducing duplication across Slack, CLI, and MCP adapters.

## Background / Why
Paperclip’s service-layer pattern keeps behavior consistent regardless of caller. Quotey currently has risks of rule logic scattering across handlers and adapters.

## Scope
- Define service interfaces for quote mutations, approvals, and governance actions
- Move shared validation/policy checks into services
- Keep transports thin: parse input, call service, render response

## Guardrails
- No behavior regressions across existing channels
- Deterministic engine decisions remain centralized

## Acceptance
- Critical workflows execute through service-layer APIs
- Duplicate rule logic in transports is removed
- Tests validate parity across Slack/CLI/MCP entrypoints

title: PC-7.3: JSONB Extensible Metadata Pattern — bounded flexibility without schema chaos
description:
## Objective
Standardize controlled JSON metadata usage for extensibility while keeping typed core fields authoritative and queryable.

## Background / Why
Paperclip uses JSONB payloads for evolvable attributes. Quotey needs similar flexibility for contextual metadata, but with strict guardrails to prevent hidden business logic.

## Scope
- Define where JSON metadata is allowed vs forbidden
- Add validation contracts and schema version tags for metadata blobs
- Provide query helpers/index strategy for high-value keys

## Guardrails
- Core pricing/policy invariants never live only inside metadata
- Metadata schema changes are versioned and documented

## Acceptance
- Metadata usage is explicit and validated
- High-value keys are queryable efficiently
- Backward compatibility strategy is documented

title: PC-7.4: Per-Org Human-Readable ID Prefixes — safer cross-system traceability
description:
## Objective
Support configurable, human-readable ID prefixes per org for quotes/approvals/artifacts so operators can reason about records across Slack, CRM, and exports quickly.

## Background / Why
Paperclip benefits from recognizable entity identifiers in multi-team operations. Quotey can reduce operational confusion and improve incident/debug speed with deterministic prefixing.

## Scope
- Define prefix config and collision-resistant sequence rules
- Apply prefixes to externally surfaced identifiers
- Preserve stable internal primary keys for referential integrity

## Guardrails
- Prefix changes cannot rewrite historical IDs
- Generated IDs remain unique and deterministic

## Acceptance
- Org-specific prefixes appear in user-facing IDs
- Sequence generation is race-safe
- Mapping between internal IDs and external IDs is auditable

title: EPIC-V2-MOBILE: Mobile Approval App (PWA)
description:
## Overview
A minimal PWA (Progressive Web App) where managers see pending approval requests, view deal context (customer history, margin impact, competitive situation), and approve/reject with one tap. Works on iOS and Android.

## Why This Matters
Deals stall because managers aren't at their desks. They're in meetings, traveling, or working remotely. Mobile approvals unblock deals faster. The #1 cause of approval delays is "manager wasn't at their computer."

## Background
v1 approvals happen in Slack. Mobile experience is poor. This adds a dedicated PWA that's optimized for mobile.

## Technical Considerations
- PWA: works on iOS Safari, Android Chrome
- Push notifications: native notifications for new approvals
- Offline support: view pending, cached data
- Auth: same Slack OAuth, or simple magic link
- UI: large touch targets, readable on small screens
- Deal context: customer history, margin, competitive info
- Quick actions: Approve, Reject, Need Info (asks rep questions)
- Snooze: remind me later

## User Experience
Manager receives push notification:
"🔔 Approval Request: Acme Corp - $21,420 (15% discount)"

Opens PWA:
```
Approval Request
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Customer: Acme Corp
Deal: $21,420/yr
Discount: 15% (exceeds 10% cap)
Margin: 62%

Context:
• 14 month customer, 98% utilization
• +50 seats expansion
• Competitor X offered 20% discount

[ Approve ] [ Reject ] [ Need Info ]
```

## Goals
1. PWA works on iOS and Android
2. Push notifications for new approvals
3. Full deal context on mobile
4. One-tap approve/reject
5. "Need Info" option
6. Offline viewing
7. Biometric auth option (FaceID/TouchID)

## Dependencies
- Slack integration (exists in v1)
- Approval workflow (exists in v1)
- Account/Deal data (exists in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #8]

title: PC-5.1: Scheduled Quote Actions — Heartbeat-style lifecycle automation
description: Implement scheduled action engine for quote lifecycle. Schema: scheduled_action (id, entity_type, entity_id, action_type [auto_follow_up/auto_expire/auto_escalate/price_refresh/reminder], fire_at TIMESTAMP, status [pending/fired/cancelled/failed], result TEXT, created_at, fired_at). Action types: follow-up if quote not viewed in 3 days, expire if not accepted in 30 days, escalate if approval pending > 4 hours, refresh prices with updated catalog nightly, remind rep of quotes expiring this week. Runner: background task checks pending actions on interval (configurable, default 5 min). Inspired by Paperclip's heartbeat scheduler (configurable interval, skip if paused/budget-exceeded).

title: TASK: Design web portal architecture
description:
## Overview
Design the web portal architecture - how it serves quotes and handles customer interactions.

## Why This Matters
Technical foundation. Need to decide on server approach, URL scheme, security model.

## Architecture Options
1. **Embedded**: Serve static HTML/JS from Quotey binary
2. **Separate**: Standalone web service
3. **Hybrid**: Embedded with optional external server

## Decision: Embedded
- Serve from Quotey binary (Axum)
- Single binary deployment
- No separate infrastructure
- Customer URLs: http://rep-machine:3847/quote/abc123

## URL Structure
- `/quote/{token}` - View quote
- `/quote/{token}/approve` - Approve
- `/quote/{token}/reject` - Reject
- `/quote/{token}/comment` - Add comment

## Security
- Token-based access (no auth)
- Tokens expire (configurable)
- Optional password for sensitive deals
- Rate limiting

title: EPIC-V3-PAPERCLIP: Governance, Operations & Data Model Overhaul
description:
## Overview

Comprehensive overhaul of quotey's governance, operational, and data model layers, inspired by deep analysis of Paperclip's AI agent orchestration platform (https://paperclip.ing, https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip).

## Why This Matters

Quotey has strong CPQ fundamentals (pricing engine, policy engine, MCP tools, negotiation autopilot, anomaly detection) but lacks the governance infrastructure needed to scale: there's no sales org hierarchy, no rep profile entity, no quote locking, no structured approval discussions, no discount budgets, no cost tracking, no scheduled actions, and the audit trail is incomplete. Paperclip solved these exact governance challenges for AI agent orchestration — we adapt their patterns for sales operations.

## Strategic Alignment

This epic directly serves quotey's core mission: 'Build a Rust, local-first CPQ agent for Slack with deterministic, auditable pricing decisions.' Every idea here strengthens auditability, governance, or operational control.

## Scope: 17 Feature Ideas Across 7 Categories

### Category 1: Entity & Data Model (6 tasks)
1.1 Multi-Tenant Org Scoping — org_id on all tables
1.2 Quote Purpose Tracing — link quotes to business objectives
1.3 Rep Profile Entity — replace bare actor_id with structured sales_rep
1.4 Quote Locking — atomic checkout prevents concurrent edits
1.5 Quote Comments/Activity Feed — structured thread per quote
1.6 Integration Adapter Pattern — pluggable CRM/notification/PDF

### Category 2: Governance & Approvals (4 tasks)
2.1 Rich Approval Types + Payload — typed approvals with context
2.2 Approval Discussion Thread — comments before decision
2.3 Board Override / Emergency Controls — sales ops controls
2.4 Org-Level Policy Toggles — runtime-configurable governance

### Category 3: Cost Tracking & Budgets (3 tasks)
3.1 AI Usage Cost Tracking Per Quote — track tokens/cost per operation
3.2 Discount Budget System — monthly discount authority per rep
3.3 Deal Cost Attribution — billing codes and channel tracking

### Category 4: Observability & Audit (3 tasks)
4.1 Enhanced Audit Trail — actor model + structured details
4.2 Live Quote Update Events — WebSocket/SSE push
4.3 Ops Dashboard Summary API — pipeline and health metrics

### Category 5: Execution & Scheduling (3 tasks)
5.1 Scheduled Quote Actions — heartbeat-style follow-ups
5.2 Stale Quote Detection — flag stuck quotes
5.3 AI Context Persistence — multi-session memory

### Category 6: Organizational Structure (3 tasks)
6.1 Sales Org Hierarchy — reports_to chain
6.2 Rep Specialization / Competency Profile — capabilities
6.3 Pricing Configuration Templates — export/import configs

### Category 7: Technical Architecture (4 tasks)
7.1 Per-Entity Schema Documentation
7.2 Service Layer Business Rules
7.3 JSONB Extensible Metadata
7.4 Per-Org Human-Readable ID Prefixes

## Dependency Waves

**Wave 0 (Foundational):** Rep Profile, Policy Toggles, Quote Locking, Quote Comments
**Wave 1 (Builds on W0):** Rich Approvals, AI Cost Tracking, Sales Org Hierarchy, Adapter Pattern
**Wave 2 (Builds on W0+W1):** Approval Discussion, Discount Budgets, Scheduled Actions, Dashboard API
**Wave 3 (Full stack):** AI Context Persistence, Multi-Tenant, Kanban UI, Config Templates

## Research Source
Full analysis: .planning/research/paperclip-analysis.md
Paperclip repo: https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip (MIT, TypeScript, PostgreSQL)

description: Enhance quotey's data model with sales-specific entities inspired by Paperclip's agent/company/issue model. Adds rep profiles, quote locking, structured comments, org scoping, goal tracing, and pluggable adapters. These are the foundational tables and domain types that the governance and operational layers build upon.

title: PC-1.1: Multi-Tenant Org Scoping — org_id isolation and data boundaries
description:
## Objective
Introduce explicit organization scoping so Quotey can safely support multiple sales organizations in a single deployment while preserving deterministic behavior and auditability.

## Background / Why
Paperclip scopes core entities by company_id; Quotey currently assumes a single implicit org context. This blocks safe expansion to multiple business units or subsidiaries and weakens data governance.

## Scope
- Add org_id to all business-domain tables that are logically tenant-owned
- Add org ownership constraints to repositories and queries
- Add deterministic default-org behavior for existing single-tenant deployments
- Add migration/backfill strategy that preserves existing records

## Guardrails
- No cross-org reads/writes in normal query paths
- Deterministic engines remain the source of truth (no LLM enforcement)
- Preserve current single-tenant UX until org switching is explicitly enabled

## Acceptance
- Tenant-owned rows are always filtered by org_id
- Existing tests still pass under default org
- New tests prove cross-org isolation

title: PC-1.2: Quote Purpose Tracing — tie quotes to strategic intent
description:
## Objective
Attach a clear business purpose to each quote (new logo, expansion, renewal rescue, competitive displacement, etc.) so decisions and outcomes can be analyzed against original intent.

## Background / Why
Paperclip links work items to explicit business goals. Quotey has quote status and pricing telemetry, but not intent provenance. This makes it harder to analyze whether policy and approval outcomes align with strategy.

## Scope
- Add quote purpose taxonomy and optional freeform rationale
- Persist purpose metadata in quote lifecycle events
- Surface purpose in approvals, analytics, and audit views

## Guardrails
- Keep taxonomy deterministic and versioned
- Do not block quote creation when purpose is unknown; capture unknown explicitly

## Acceptance
- Every quote has a purpose state (known or unknown)
- Purpose is queryable in analytics and audit paths
- Purpose changes are append-only in audit history

title: TASK: Define anomaly detection rules
description:
## Overview
Define the specific anomaly detection rules and thresholds.

## Why This Matters
Foundation. What constitutes an anomaly?

## Anomaly Types & Rules

### Discount Anomaly
- Flag if: discount > 2 * customer_avg + 1 std_dev
- Warning threshold: 1.5 * avg
- Critical threshold: 3 * avg

### Margin Anomaly
- Flag if: margin < product_category_floor
- Warning: floor + 5%
- Critical: below floor

### Config Anomaly
- Flag if: quantity > 3 * customer_avg
- Flag if: new product (first time ordered)

### Price Anomaly
- Flag if: total > 1.5 * similar_deals_avg
- Similar = same products + segment + region

[Created from: ultrathink - EPIC-V2-ANOMALY subtask]
close_reason: Defined deterministic anomaly detection threshold rules with tests and planning spec

title: EPIC: Real-component test coverage + full E2E integration observability program
description:
Objective: close critical QA gaps by replacing fake/in-memory critical-path reliance with real-component tests and delivering deterministic end-to-end integration scripts with high-fidelity structured logging.

Evidence observed in repo:
- Widespread fake/in-memory markers across crates (qa-tag: fake-in-memory-critical-path).
- Existing e2e bootstrap script exists but no complete, operator-grade end-to-end scenario runner with rich per-step diagnostics.
- Planning docs already identify this risk area (e.g., QA fake inventory + premortem).

Done criteria for epic:
1) Critical-path unit/integration coverage uses real repositories/components by default, with explicit and reviewed exceptions.
2) Full scenario E2E suite exists (net-new, renewal, discount exception, failure/recovery), executable via scripts, with structured logs and persisted artifacts.
3) CI gates enforce coverage, no-fake policy on critical paths, and deterministic scenario replay diagnostics.
close_reason: Program complete: Tracks A-D all closed with real-component coverage, deterministic E2E harness+diff reporting, CI quality gates, and QA dashboard artifacts

title: TASK: Design analytics metrics and dimensions
description:
## Overview
Define the metrics and dimensions for the analytics dashboard.

## Why This Matters
Foundation. What are we measuring?

## Metrics

### Pricing Health
- Average discount by segment
- Average discount by rep
- Discount distribution (% in each bucket)
- Average margin by product
- Margin distribution

### Velocity
- Quote creation to close (days)
- Quote age (in flight)
- Approval turnaround time
- Time by stage

### Products
- Quote count by product
- Attach rate (bundles/add-ons)
- Popular bundles

### Approvals
- Approval rate
- Rejection rate
- Auto-approval rate
- Bottleneck analysis (who delays)

### Deals
- Deal size distribution
- Win rate by price range
- Win rate by segment

## Dimensions
- Date (week, month, quarter, year)
- Segment (SMB, Mid-Market, Enterprise)
- Region (US, EU, APAC)
- Rep
- Product category

title: EPIC-V2-PORTAL: Web Quote Portal
description:
## Overview
A lightweight web UI alongside Slack - not a full dashboard, just a shareable link customers can view interactively, approve electronically (e-signature capture), and download PDFs from. Replaces clumsy PDF attachments with a modern experience.

## Why This Matters
PDFs are static and clumsy. Customers can't easily ask questions or approve. A web portal allows interactive quote exploration, shows pricing breakdowns, lets customers compare options, and captures explicit approval with timestamp. Complements Slack without replacing it - the quote lives in Slack, but the customer experience is web-native.

## Background
Sales reps currently send PDFs via email which get lost in inboxes. Customers can't easily compare options or ask clarifications. This creates friction in the close process. A web portal solves this while maintaining the Slack-first workflow for reps.

## Technical Considerations
- Minimal web server (Axum in Rust, or embedded static files)
- Shareable links with expiration (configurable, default 30 days)
- No authentication required for customers (link-based access)
- Optional: password protection for sensitive deals
- Electronic signature capture (simple click-to-approve + timestamp)
- Customer can add comments/questions on specific line items
- Mobile-responsive design
- All data served from local SQLite (no external dependencies)

## User Experience
Rep clicks "Share Link" in Slack, system generates unique URL. Customer receives link, can:
- View full quote with pricing breakdown
- Compare different configuration options
- Ask questions (goes back to rep via Slack)
- Approve electronically (triggers workflow in Quotey)
- Download PDF

## Goals
1. Generate secure shareable links for quotes
2. Interactive quote viewer with pricing breakdown
3. Customer can approve/reject with electronic signature
4. Customer can comment on specific line items
5. Links expire after configurable duration

## Dependencies
- PDF generation (exists in v1)
- Slack integration (exists in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #3]

title: TASK: Define MCP tool schema for Quotey
description:
## Overview
Define the MCP tool schema - what tools/actions Quotey exposes to AI agents.

## Why This Matters
The schema defines the contract between Quotey and AI agents. Must be comprehensive yet simple. Each tool needs clear inputs/outputs.

1. `catalog.search(query)` - Search products
2. `catalog.get(product_id)` - Get product details
3. `quote.create(customer_id, lines)` - Create new quote
4. `quote.get(quote_id)` - Get quote details
5. `quote.price(quote_id)` - Price a quote
6. `quote.approve(quote_id)` - Request approval
7. `quote.status(quote_id)` - Get approval status
8. `quote.pdf(quote_id)` - Generate PDF
9. `quote.list(filters)` - List quotes
10. `approval.pending()` - Get pending approvals

## Output
JSON schema document for each tool with:
- Tool name and description
- Input parameters and types
- Output format
- Error cases

title: PC-5.3: AI Context Persistence — deterministic multi-session memory
description:
## Objective
Persist bounded AI context across quote sessions so follow-up conversations and negotiations remain coherent without sacrificing determinism or auditability.

## Background / Why
Paperclip preserves operational context across runs; Quotey currently has limited long-horizon conversational memory. Strong context persistence improves user experience and downstream policy decision quality.

## Scope
- Define structured session memory artifacts (facts, assumptions, unresolved questions)
- Persist and retrieve context by quote/thread/session boundaries
- Enforce TTL and pruning policies to prevent unbounded drift

## Guardrails
- Memory is advisory, not authoritative for pricing/policy
- Every persisted context mutation is auditable

## Acceptance
- Session context can be restored after restart
- Retrieval is deterministic and bounded
- Tests cover stale-memory expiration and conflict handling

title: EPIC-V2-PDF: Branded PDF Templates + White-Label
description:
## Overview
Ship with 5 professional quote templates (executive summary, detailed line items, product comparison, renewal, compact) with customizable branding. Users upload logo, customize colors, set as defaults. White-label option for partners/resellers.

## Why This Matters
The quote IS the product. It represents your company to the customer. Professional, branded quotes build trust. Different templates for different situations (executive summary for quick decisions, detailed for procurement).

## Background
v1 has basic PDF generation. This adds multiple templates and customization.

## Technical Considerations
- Template engine: Tera/Handlebars (already in use)
- 5 templates:
  1. Executive Summary: 1-page, high-level, for quick decisions
  2. Detailed: full line items, all pricing breakdowns
  3. Comparison: side-by-side product/price options
  4. Renewal: shows current→proposed changes
  5. Compact: minimal, for emails
- Customization: logo upload, primary/secondary colors, company info
- White-label: custom sender name/logo, remove Quotey branding
- Template selection: default per quote type, overridable
- PDF optimization: proper font embedding, vector graphics

## User Experience
Admin goes to "/quotey templates" in Slack:
- View current templates
- Upload logo (drag & drop)
- Set primary color (color picker)
- Preview each template
- Set defaults by quote type

Rep generating PDF:
```
[ Generate PDF (Executive Summary) ]
[ Generate PDF (Detailed) ]
[ Generate PDF (Comparison) ]
[ Customize Template ]
```

## Goals
1. 5 professional templates
2. Logo upload and customization
3. Color scheme customization
4. Company info customization
5. White-label option
6. Default template per quote type
7. PDF quality: proper fonts, vectors

## Dependencies
- PDF generation (exists in v1)
- Template engine (exists in v1)
- File upload (Slack - exists in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #7]

title: PC-5.2: Stale Quote Detection — Flag stuck quotes and trigger action
description: Detect and surface stale quotes. Rules: draft > 7 days -> flag stale, sent but not viewed > 5 days -> trigger follow-up, approval pending > SLA threshold -> auto-escalate, negotiation idle > 48 hours -> auto-close with reason. Weekly 'health check' report to sales ops via Slack. Stale count on dashboard. Inspired by Paperclip's orphan/stale detection: 'Surface problems, don't hide them' — dashboards surface stale tasks rather than auto-repairing.

description:
## Overview
Design Progressive Web App architecture for mobile approvals.

## Why This Matters
Foundation. How PWA connects to Quotey?

- Static HTML/JS/CSS served from Quotey
- API calls back to Quotey for data
- Service worker for offline
- Web App Manifest

- `/approvals` - List pending
- `/approvals/:id` - Detail view
- `/settings` - Preferences

## Tech Stack
- Vanilla JS (no framework for simplicity)
- Tailwind CSS (lightweight)
- Service worker for offline

title: PC-6.2: Rep Specialization & Competency Profiles — capability-aware routing
description:

Model rep capabilities (industry expertise, product depth, approval authority) so workflow routing and assistance can leverage explicit competencies.

## Background / Why
Paperclip’s agent capability metadata drives safer task assignment. Quotey can use similar structures for approval routing, escalation, and AI guidance quality.

## Scope
- Define competency schema and versioned taxonomy
- Attach competency metadata to sales_rep profiles
- Integrate competencies into routing and recommendation decisions

## Guardrails
- Capability metadata augments but does not override hard policy rules
- Avoid freeform entropy by constraining allowed capability keys

## Acceptance
- Competencies are stored and queryable
- Routing logic can reference competencies deterministically
- Backfill path exists for current reps

- Rollback metadata must be captured in audit trail

## Acceptance
- Templates round-trip without semantic drift
- Dry-run reports all changes before apply
- Rollback restores prior state deterministically

title: Security: Add length limits to quote ID extraction regex to prevent ReDoS
close_reason:
Added length limits to all ID extraction and input processing functions across 4 crates:

1. slack/socket.rs: quote_id_from_text() — added MAX_QUOTE_ID_LEN (64) check before normalization
2. slack/commands.rs: normalize_quote_command() and parse_quote_command() — added MAX_COMMAND_INPUT_LEN (2048) input guard
3. agent/runtime.rs: extract_prefixed_token() — added MAX_ID_TOKEN_LEN (64) check; classify_thread_intent() — added MAX_INPUT_TEXT_LEN (4096) truncation
4. mcp/server.rs: extract_quote_id_from_arguments() — added MAX_QUOTE_ID_LEN (64) filter; normalize_id() — added length check

Note: No regex crate is used in the codebase, so traditional ReDoS is not applicable. The real risk was unbounded string processing from crafted input, which is now mitigated.

parse_quote_id_token() in slack/commands.rs already had a strict 11-char exact-length check — no change needed.

720 workspace tests pass, clippy clean.

3. agent/runtime.rs: extract_prefixed_token() — added MAX_ID_TOKEN_LEN (64) check; classify_thread_intent() — added MAX_INPUT_TEXT_LEN (4096) truncation
4. mcp/server.rs: extract_quote_id_from_arguments() — added MAX_QUOTE_ID_LEN (64) filter; normalize_id() — added length check

Note: No regex crate is used in the codebase, so traditional ReDoS is not applicable. The real risk was unbounded string processing from crafted input, which is now mitigated.

parse_quote_id_token() in slack/commands.rs already had a strict 11-char exact-length check — no change needed.

title: EPIC-V2-RULES: Visual No-Code Rule Builder
description:
## Overview
A Slack modal or CLI tool that lets sales ops admins build pricing rules and constraints visually - dropdowns, condition builders, formula editors - without writing SQL. Exports directly to SQLite.

## Why This Matters
Current Quotey requires SQL knowledge to manage pricing rules and constraints. This limits who can make changes to developers only. Sales ops need to be able to adjust discount caps, add new pricing tiers, and modify constraints without waiting for engineering. Democratizes setup to the people who understand the business.

## Background
In v1, pricing rules, constraints, and policies are stored in SQLite but require SQL to manage. This creates a bottleneck. A visual builder lets sales ops make changes quickly while ensuring data integrity.

## Technical Considerations
- Slack modal UI for rule building
- Alternative: CLI interactive wizard
- Condition builder: field selector → operator → value
- Formula editor with variable picker
- Preview what rule will look like in SQL
- Validation: test rule against sample data before saving
- Version control: changes tracked in audit log
- Import/export rules as JSON (for backup/versioning)

## Rule Types to Support
1. Pricing rules (base prices, volume tiers, formulas)
2. Discount policies (caps by segment, product, deal size)
3. Constraint rules (requires, excludes, attribute constraints)
4. Approval thresholds (by discount %, deal value, margin)

## User Experience
Admin opens "/quotey rules" in Slack, selects rule type, fills in form:
- Rule name: "SMB Discount Cap"
- Condition: "customer_segment = SMB AND deal_size < 10000"
- Action: "max_discount = 10%"
- Preview shows affected quotes
- Save creates/updates rule in SQLite

## Goals
1. Visual builder for pricing rules
2. Visual builder for constraint rules
3. Visual builder for discount policies
4. Visual builder for approval thresholds
5. Preview and test before save
6. Audit trail of all changes

## Dependencies
- Slack integration (exists in v1)
- SQLite schema for rules (exists in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #4]

title: TASK: Design rule builder UI schema
description:
## Overview
Design the data schema for visual rule building - how rules are represented.

## Why This Matters
Foundation for builder UI. Must support all rule types and be serializable to SQLite.

## Schema Design
```rust
struct VisualRule {
    name: String,
    rule_type: RuleType, // pricing, constraint, policy, threshold
    conditions: Vec<Condition>,
    actions: Vec<Action>,
    metadata: RuleMetadata,
}

struct Condition {
    field: String,
    operator: Operator, // equals, not_equals, greater_than, etc.
    value: serde_json::Value,
    connector: Option<Connector>, // and, or
}

struct Action {
    action_type: ActionType,
    parameters: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
}
```

title: Security: Audit and fix potential SQL injection vectors in repository layer
close_reason:
Comprehensive SQL injection audit completed. 400+ SQL query locations analyzed across all 7 crates. Findings:
- NO critical/high severity SQL injection vulnerabilities found
- All user-facing endpoints (portal, MCP, CRM) use parameterized queries consistently
- Dynamic WHERE clause construction (crm.rs, quote.rs) uses hardcoded column names with bind parameters for values — SAFE
- FTS5 search (product.rs) properly quote-wraps and binds user input — SAFE
- Analytics query builder uses &'static str for all SQL fragments — SAFE

One improvement made: Refactored fixtures.rs sql_array_from_ids() from format!-based SQL string interpolation to parameterized bind queries (delete_by_ids + bind_placeholders helpers). While only used with compile-time constants, this eliminates the pattern as a copy-paste risk.

Also fixed: quotey-agent Cargo.toml — moved tokio from dev-deps to deps (was causing compilation failure).

717 workspace tests pass, clippy clean.

title: EPIC-V2-AUTOQUOTE: Auto-Quote from Email/RFP
description:
## Overview
Drop an email or paste an RFP text, and the system extracts requirements, maps them to products, identifies ambiguities that need clarification, and drafts a complete quote. Rep reviews and tweaks before sending.

## Why This Matters
This is THE key differentiator. Reps spend hours manually parsing customer requests, RFPs, and emails to build quotes. This does it in seconds. The #1 blocker to CPQ adoption is data entry time - this eliminates it.

## Background
Already planned in PROJECT.md as "Quote Intelligence" but needs emphasis. This feature takes unstructured input (email, RFP, Slack thread) and converts it to structured quote data using LLM extraction + product matching.

## Technical Considerations
- Email parser: extract requirements from plain text emails
- RFP parser: more complex, multi-section document with tables
- Slack thread parser: conversation history → requirements
- Product matching: fuzzy name matching + attribute mapping
- Ambiguity detection: flag items that need human clarification
- Draft builder: create quote in "draft" state for review
- Confidence scoring: show how confident the system is

## User Experience
Rep pastes RFP text into Slack:
```
/quote parse-rfp
[PASTE RFP CONTENT]
```

System responds:
```
📋 Draft Quote Created

Extracted Requirements:
- 150 user seats ✓
- SSO integration → matched to "SSO Add-on" (95% confidence)
- 24/7 support → matched to "Premium Support" (98% confidence)
- Annual billing → understood
- Need: clarification on "enterprise tier" - did they mean Pro or Enterprise plan?

[ View Draft ] [ Edit ] [ Confirm ]
```

## Goals
1. Parse email → requirements
2. Parse RFP → requirements (with table support)
3. Match requirements to products automatically
4. Flag ambiguities for human clarification
5. Draft quote creation for review
6. Confidence scoring on matches

## Dependencies
- LLM integration (exists in v1)
- Product catalog (exists in v1)
- Quote creation flow (exists in v1)

[Created from: ultrathink feature ideation - TOP 10 IDEA #5]

Database Error

title: Reliability: Add exponential backoff overflow protection in slack socket
close_reason:
Added overflow protection to ReconnectPolicy backoff in slack/socket.rs:

1. MAX_RETRIES_CAP (100): effective_max_retries() clamps max_retries to prevent infinite reconnect loops from misconfigured values
2. MIN_BASE_DELAY_MS (50): backoff() enforces minimum base delay to prevent tight reconnect loops even when base_delay_ms=0
3. max_delay_ms floor: ensured max >= base so the cap never results in shorter-than-base delays
4. Removed if !delay.is_zero() guard (now always sleeps since minimum delay is guaranteed)

Existing protections preserved: attempt.min(16) caps exponent, saturating_mul prevents multiplication overflow, .min(max_delay_ms) caps final delay.

122 slack tests pass, 720 workspace tests pass, clippy clean.

title: PC-3.2: Discount Budget System — Monthly discount authority per rep
description: Monthly discount budget tracking per rep/team/org. Each rep gets discount_budget_monthly_cents on sales_rep table. Track spent_discount_cents (reset monthly). Soft alert at 80%: 'You have used 80% of your discount authority.' Hard limit at 100%: requires manager approval for any further discounts. Manager has team budget; VP has org budget. Functions: check_discount_budget(rep_id, proposed_discount_cents) -> BudgetStatus { Ok/SoftWarn/HardLimit }. Dashboard: discount budget utilization by rep/team/org. Prevents end-of-quarter margin erosion. Inspired by Paperclip's 80/100 budget pattern.
